review_title: Baserow functional modern database approach
author: Domingo A.
pros: I probably like the functionality and having the ability to self host the product is very important to be able to fully customize and align it with the institution's requirement
cons: The only thing we are missing is the live data view. For example is the live connection between airtable/google sheet existing table where we can see the changes to the original table in real time
author_duration: Used the software for: Less than 6 months

review_title: Highly recommended visual database for long-term use
description: I enjoy using Baserow and am happy to be part of the community. Open-sourcing your own product is not always easy in terms of intellectual property and old-school business mindset. I've migrated from Airtable/SmartSuite to Baserow and use Baserow for summary and visual data management that works well with other databases like Cloudflare D1 and MongoDB (integrated with Make.com). When it comes to Application Builder that the Baserow team is currently working on, according to their webinar, they plan to integrate other data sources and to develop a chart/graph feature, so I'm looking forward to using it. (Competitors/alternatives could be not only Airtable, but also Retool and Softr) Overall, Baserow is a cost-effective and strategic solution for your digital transformation.
author: Mana K.
pros: - Cost effective from free to 5 USD per month - Simple and clean interface - Simple but useful API endpoints - Nice and supportive community - Application builder is evolving now - Open-source mindset and movement
cons: - Application builder could have more feature (expectation for the future) - Filters should be sent as POST request rather than a GET parameter
author_duration: Used the software for: Less than 6 months

review_title: Great effort. needs many improvements.
description: over all support is very good. sadly, without the certain features we cannot sign up. in order for baserow to excel, it must: offer all the airtable features, for less $, or offer better platform for the same (or more) $.
author: mazin M.
pros: clean interface. seems to load much faster than airtable. support is by far exceeds others.
cons: Baserow, is missing many features offered by airtable. Such as the view imbed feature, where we can imbed a certain view, with automatically generated I frame, with options to convert the "grid" view into card format, (horizontal), that's very pleasing to look at. another, is hidden items. with baserow there is no option to hide items in order to minimize "item headers", but have all item's fully visible in expanded mode. in base row hidden items remain hidden, which makes the entire feature worthless. also, the controls, are as worthless as airtable, and are not compatible with mobile devices, in imbed / I Frame mode. Serious IOs compatibility as well, just like the competition.
author_duration: Used the software for: 6-12 months

review_title: Best Open Source Self-Hosted Airtable Alternative
description: Perfect solution for a small business that needs a database, wants an inexpensive and scalable solution and that wants to control its data.
author: Daniel R.
pros: If you want the ease of use of solutions like Airtable but at the same time control over your data and no vendor lock-in, look no further than Baserow!
cons: The product is stable but not fully mature yet. Do not expect perfect UI/UX that other solutions like Rows or Airtable might offer.
author_duration: Used the software for: 6-12 months

review_title: Build a database without programming skills
description: Great, we are so thankful that this is available free of charge.
author: Tuula R.
pros: My [sensitive content hidden] and I were in need of a low-cost solution for managing thousands of data points to track seed accessions for ecological restoration. We were losing data with Google sheets and unable to cross-reference collection data, seed farming and overall lifecycle of seed lots. We tried a costly database used by large-scale seed farmers and it didn't meet our needs; it was slow, the interface was ancient and the workflow was not intuitive. Baserow is elegant, super fast and intuitive to use.
cons: There is a learning curve; however compared to the time we would have invested in learning Python or another programming language to custom-build a database, this is much faster.
author_duration: Used the software for: Less than 6 months

review_title: Why did I ever use Airtable?
description: Great for managing all the data in dev and organize it before putting it in our primary database.
author: Marshall R.
pros: Infinite rows. Needed in products like these.
cons: The load time, the lack of Google cloud setup support, every so often it'll be buggy and I'm not sure what resources to upgrade - does it need more memory? A larger machine? Another instance? That feedback would be remarkable.
author_duration: Used the software for: Less than 6 months

review_title: Baserow: The Low Code Base we all wanted
description: Very good. I'm rooting for this platform to succeed in the long run.
author: Jacob B.
pros: I like that it is using a FOSS model, and features self-hosting. The UI is much more laid back compared to competitors like SmartSuite and Airtable. Which can be a bit much with the UI colors. Baserow has the cleanness of a tool like Notion which I prefer.Also, the templates are really good. Would love to be able to help expand them.Love how the forms work as well as public sharing, it's just better than Airtable's setup here. Something nice about the cloud-based version.
cons: The user access is really bad. There should be a way to share a single base without everything in the workspace without having to go to the highest plan. Even then going through teams only for that kind of access is archaic and harder for users to manage. I have files I'd like only certain people to access, and sometimes on an individual basis. I would love it if Baserow had a feature to let guests edit public files without an account. Similar to a form.Wish it had more "extension" features, maybe a click-up or notion direct integration. Zapier helps here, though, I want to use this as a database inside of other tools directly. There are a lot of systems that do project management very well and it would be better to use this inside of those than lock it as a competitor of those systems to "compete". The pricing model is what it is, but I don't like it. A huge reason of why I've switched out of Airtable was cost. It seemed like you kept upgrading and upgrading and still were missing key features behind a payroll. For a team that got expensive. Baserow is much cheaper, but I'd feel more inclined to pay for the premium if I got more for it. Not just one user, not all of these scope limitations, a real value add for an org. This software is competing against google sheets which works just as well for 90+% of people with the same use cases for absolutely no cost. Figuring out how much you're going to pay for a team with most of these base software's is going to require thi
author_duration: Used the software for: 6-12 months

review_title: Use Baserow instead of Google Form
author: Yoann C.
pros: easy to deployeasy to useeasy to understand
cons: we have to replace Google Forms and it cannot do it
author_duration: Used the software for: 1-2 years

review_title: Affordable and Growing alternative to Airtable and Notion
description: Overall, I had a solid experience using Baserow and I would recommend it to anyone coming from a background of using Notion or Airtable. I had connection issues with Notion and Airtable where I am from. However, I could easily fix that problem by hosting an instance of Baserow in my own server.
author: Zachary S.
pros: I think the most I liked about Baserow was that it offered a lot of the features I was expecting coming from using Notion. I also worked with Airtable before and I think that Baserow can substitute both those platforms. But it exceeds them because Baserow is open source. That's what I like about this app. You can host it wherever you want and you get a solid user experience. I certainly see myself using this app for my day to day needs and for my clients as well. Also preparing templates with this app is easy and I would definitely pursue that angle as well.
cons: I feel like a rich text field like HTML or Markdown is missing in Baserow. I saw it in their development roadmap and I can't wait to also see that field in the list of fields Baserow offers. This is important to me because with this field, I won't have to rely on google docs, or uploading markdown files to the attachment field for writing longer form content.
author_duration: Used the software for: I used a free trial

review_title: Easiest way to set up a database
author: Cameron P.
pros: I love that I can have a database created without much work and it also has a full fledged API available for me to use the data in an app.
cons: Something I'd love to see in Baserow is more customizations with the CSS of the App Builder. That'd make it so much better
author_duration: Used the software for: Less than 6 months

review_title: ChatADV e o Baserow simplificando a criação de micros-saas
description: Minha experiência tem sido excelente, haja vista a simplicidade e potência que a ferramenta possibilita, principalmente no começo do projeto, sendo acessível de fácil instalar e bastante robusta e segura.
author: Paulo Sérgio D.
pros: Ferramenta simples de instalar e configurar, além de rodar super leve e ter um dashboard amigável, possibilitando
cons: A taxa de escrita ainda me parece um pouco baixa, pois percebo um atraso maior quando aumentada.
author_duration: Used the software for: Less than 6 months

review_title: Great option for quickly turning ideas into reality
description: For most of what I've tried in Baserow it just works the way I'd expect it to work. Also, there have been multiple times where I've wished for a feature in my head and then shortly after it's been implemented.
author: Brandon A.
pros: I love the flexibility and the ease of implementing basic relationships between tables, calculated fields, and different field types.
cons: When using the API, the field names are very cryptic and I haven't figured out a way to make them more readable.
author_duration: Used the software for: Less than 6 months

review_title: Great open source alternative to AirTable
description: Good product to use for project management if you're going to get the paid tier but it's also just a solid AirTable alternative if you need a quick database for small integrations.
author: Paul P.
pros: The free tier is super generous, making it an easy recommendation for many clients to just test the waters first before committing to the product full on. It has support for many types of fields but we're waiting on a few more to be released!
cons: Kanban isn't available on the free tier, making it difficult to use it on small unpaid projects for planning.
author_duration: Used the software for: Less than 6 months

review_title: A great basic product that needs slight polishing to be outstanding
description: The basic functions and overall app design are excellent. The migration from other apps, and the creation of new instances, has been effortless.
author: Brian T.
pros: Easy migration from other applications and the lean and clean interface. The support has been outstanding.
cons: The worst feature by far is typography. The emphasis is on the structure rather than the content. For example, the Field names are Black and Bold and prominent, while the content is grey and subsidiary. The lack of any typographic controls is almost a deal breaker.
author_duration: Used the software for: 6-12 months

review_title: Best database
description: Nothing to complain about, it serves me very well!
author: Felipe Nunes S.
pros: Ease of use and integration with other software.
cons: I like the interface that is much easier to use than a conventional database system
author_duration: Used the software for: 6-12 months

review_title: Exploring my Baserom
author: Kim M.
pros: Easy to use and user friendly Self-hosted
cons: on self-hosted complex to set-up many services dependencies
author_duration: Used the software for: I used a free trial

review_title: Best nocode lowcode product
author: Verified Reviewer
pros: Fantastic product plus apis. Very useful
cons: No ROLE BASED ACCESS CONTROL in the free version
author_duration: Used the software for: 1-2 years

review_title: Capable open-source product for rapid integrations
author: Frank S.
pros: In olden times, one would build integration around spreadsheets with technologies like Visual Basic and such. Filemaker is another piece of software that comes to mind. These projects often grow to form the backbone of small-medium sized companies. While the use case is completely legitimate, it can be frustrating to see the vendor lock-in and potential tech debt this causes. Baserow is open source, really nice and accessible from a user perspective and yields the more modern integration landscape of a web application / REST API.
cons: Functionality is sometimes a little too basic, having page-sized print layouts would enable many more use cases.
author_duration: Used the software for: Less than 6 months

review_title: Perfect System
author: Pedro F.
pros: Is great system for use, like reccomend.
cons: Some users may find limitations in the platform's customizability or extensibility to meet their specific business needs.
author_duration: Used the software for: Less than 6 months

review_title: Review of Baserow
author: Jan B.
pros: I think is very easy to use overall compared to other platforms
cons: There is actually little to complain about. Customer service could be a little better
author_duration: Used the software for: I used a free trial

review_title: Works great
author: Josiah P.
pros: Simple and intuitive. Focused feature set
cons: Needs some more work in a few areas: UX, api, and no code integrations
author_duration: Used the software for: Less than 6 months

review_title: Johan Reviewb
description: pretty good we work on for scool and compagny project
author: johan K.
pros: Excel Like, but some features was missing when i used i. Like date formule
cons: that is the same work as excel. I would like to make some BI with it but currently this is absent
author_duration: Used the software for: I used a free trial

review_title: Great software
author: Tymoteusz J.
pros: Views are great feature for me - I'm sharing some of my tables publicly.
cons: I'm waiting for 2FA feature to increase security
author_duration: Used the software for: Less than 6 months

review_title: Baserow is excellent
description: Overall the experience is very positive and there are people behind committed to making Baserow better.
author: Cyrill H.
pros: Baserow is simply excellent and we use it to manage all our marketing in one central place. It offers us an easy way to create a database, collect data and trigger automations via Make.com. Its a great and better alternative than Airtable. Also the fact that there is a self-hosted option, make this really attractive. We keep discovering new ways of using Baserow as we go, and we plan to use it as our central database to manage all kinds of activities.
cons: At some times it might be a bit cumbersome to understand how it works or to find the exact solution to your problem. However, we kept trying and thanks to the Baserow Community and their Support we always could figure it out.
author_duration: Used the software for: 6-12 months

review_title: The best database
description: Excellent application for small and medium.business as well as new developers
author: Jader T.
pros: Really great tool due to its simplicty and also the constant evolution. Simce the start ive been excited about that
cons: Baserow still does not allow copying bases
author_duration: Used the software for: 1-2 years
